title: Dogfood
use_when: Use Dogfood when a web UI, local app, staging URL, or production URL needs browser-driven exploratory QA that exercises the product like a real user and captures pages tested, flows tested, console errors, screenshots, issue severity, blockers, and release evidence.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Dogfood for non-UI code review, unit-test-only verification, data validation, product strategy, PRD drafting, release coordination, or incident root-cause debugging; route those to eng-review, qa, validate-data, product-review, release, or systematic-debugging instead.
output_artifact: Dogfood QA report with pages tested, flows tested, console errors, screenshots, severity table, blockers, and release decision
framework_fit: Dogfood fits Delivery work in the Launch & Learn lifecycle when observable web-product behavior needs evidence from realistic browser use before QA pass, release readiness, or production smoke-check decisions. Use it to produce a concrete QA report with reproducible findings, not a generic testing checklist.
failure_modes: Reports a pass without naming the pages tested, flows tested, console errors checked, screenshots or other evidence captured, and explicit blockers or untested areas.
Stays at a static review level instead of navigating the app, exercising real user flows, checking the browser console after interactions, and collecting evidence for every issue.
Treats every issue as equal priority instead of classifying severity, category, release impact, owner, repro steps, expected behavior, and actual behavior.

examples.prompt: Use $dogfood to test this local web app end-to-end: map the sitemap, exercise the signup and dashboard flows, check console errors, capture screenshots, and report blockers.
examples.expected_artifact: Dogfood QA report with pages tested, flows tested, console errors, screenshots, severity table, blockers, and release decision
